why: The webhook_run_flow handler has no standard auth dependency (no Depends(get_current_active_user) or Depends(api_key_security) in its decorator or signature); it calls get_webhook_user which may allow unauthenticated execution depending on auth settings, making flow execution reachable without verified identity.
The create_upload_file endpoint has no authentication dependency in its decorator or parameters, allowing any unauthenticated caller to upload files associated with an arbitrary flow_id.

The file is saved via save_uploaded_file with folder_name derived from flow_id_str (a UUID string conversion), but the uploaded file's original filename is passed without any containment check against a fixed base path, potentially allowing path traversal if save_uploaded_file uses the original filename unsafely.

The build_flow endpoint decorator has no dependencies=[Depends(get_current_active_user)] listed, and while CurrentActiveUser is present as a parameter, the endpoint could be reached and the flow existence check occurs before auth is validated depending on FastAPI dependency resolution order — more critically, this endpoint is not guarded the same way as retrieve_vertices_order which explicitly lists the dependency in the decorator.

raw_code.code is caller-supplied and passed directly to Component(_code=raw_code.code) which then goes into build_custom_component_template, a function that compiles and executes the user-provided Python code string without any sandbox or allowlist restriction.

code_request.code is caller-supplied and passed directly into Component(_code=code_request.code) then built via build_custom_component_template, executing arbitrary user-provided Python code without sandboxing.

The build_public_tmp endpoint has no authentication dependency in its decorator and relies solely on a client_id cookie plus a public-flow database check, meaning any unauthenticated caller can trigger flow execution including arbitrary component graphs.

download_image accepts a caller-supplied flow_id and file_name with no authentication dependency (no get_current_active_user or equivalent), allowing any unauthenticated user to retrieve files from any flow's storage by guessing or enumerating IDs.

full_file_name is constructed using the caller-supplied file.filename without sanitizing path separators or traversal sequences, so a filename containing '../' could escape the intended flow storage folder when passed to storage_service.save_file.

The containment check uses startswith on a string representation of the resolved path, which can be bypassed if allowed_base is a prefix of another directory name (e.g., /base/profile_pictures vs /base/profile_pictures_evil); os.path.commonpath would be a safer check.

The auto_login endpoint issues long-term tokens and sets session cookies without any authentication dependency or current-user check; only an application-level flag (AUTO_LOGIN) gates it, meaning any unauthenticated caller can obtain a valid session when auto-login is enabled.

exec() is called on a dynamically compiled function body that embeds input_.display_name values (from Vertex objects whose display_name may originate from user-controlled flow definitions), which could allow injection of arbitrary Python code into the generated function string.

The /refresh POST endpoint reads the refresh_token cookie and issues new access/refresh tokens without any current-user dependency or explicit auth check validating the caller's identity beyond the cookie value itself, which is processed by create_refresh_token but the handler has no Depends(get_current_active_user) guard.

The /logout POST endpoint clears authentication cookies without any current-user dependency or auth check, meaning any unauthenticated request can invoke it (though impact is low, it is a sensitive auth operation with no authorization guard).

order_params['column'] is passed to getattr(Flow, ...) to dynamically select a sort column from a caller-supplied dict; while getattr with a model attribute is not raw SQL, a caller controlling the 'column' key could potentially reference unexpected model attributes, and the 'direction' key is dispatched through SORT_DISPATCHER without visible validation of the column name against an allowlist.

Same as unit 18: order_params dict with caller-supplied 'column' and 'direction' keys is used to dynamically select ORM attributes and sort direction without an explicit allowlist check on the column name.
